items.title: Adopt `src/` for any project beyond a single file
items.avoid: Flat layout for anything that has tests, CI, or will be installed by users вЂ” the ambient cwd-based imports hide packaging bugs.
items.prefer: Scaffold `src/<pkg>/__init__.py`, `pyproject.toml`, and `tests/` from day one; install with `pip install -e .`.
items.why: The cost is one extra install step; the payoff is layout-induced correctness вЂ” tests and CI run against the installed package, exactly like users.
items.example:
```
mypkg/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ pyproject.toml
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ src/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ mypkg/__init__.py
в””в”Ђв”Ђ tests/test_smoke.py
```

items.title: Pair `src/` with editable installs in CI
items.avoid: Running tests in CI from the source tree without installing the package first.
items.why: CI then tests exactly what users will install вЂ” same import path, same metadata, same surfaces.
items.example:
```yaml
steps:
  - run: pip install -e ".[dev]"
  - run: pytest -q
```

items.title: Don't add `src/` to `sys.path` manually
items.avoid: `sys.path.insert(0, 'src')` in `conftest.py` to 'make imports work without installing'.
items.prefer: Run `pip install -e .` once; let Python find the package the normal way.
items.why: Manual path manipulation re-creates the flat-layout trap вЂ” ambient imports work locally but break for any user who actually installs the package.
items.example: If you're tempted to write `sys.path.insert(0, 'src')`, fix the install instead.

items.title: Keep tests in `tests/` at the repo root (a sibling of `src/`)
items.avoid: Placing tests inside `src/<pkg>/tests/` where they could be shipped in the wheel.
items.prefer: Top-level `tests/` directory; tests import the package as a user would (`from mypkg.x import y`).
items.why: Tests are not part of what ships. Keeping them outside `src/` prevents accidental shipping and keeps the import direction one-way.
items.example:
```
src/mypkg/...   # ships
tests/test_x.py # does not ship
```

items.title: Verify wheel contents before publishing
items.avoid: Uploading to PyPI without inspecting the artefact вЂ” extra files or wrong layout slip through silently.
items.prefer: `python -m build && unzip -l dist/*.whl && twine check dist/*` as a pre-publish ritual.
items.why: The wheel is the user-facing artefact; confirming it once locally avoids yanked releases later.
items.example:
```bash
python -m build
unzip -l dist/mypkg-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl | grep mypkg
twine check dist/*
```
